model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current run already improved optimization dynamics, so the next low-complexity gain is to regularize the logits directly. Mild label smoothing often improves CIFAR-10 validation loss under short training budgets even when accuracy changes little, which matches this evaluator’s objective.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The last regularization change was harmful, so this candidate removes it and tests a cleaner optimizer variant instead. AdamW often gives better early-budget generalization than Adam with the same schedule because decoupled weight decay interacts more predictably with short cosine runs.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The optimizer and budget-aware schedule are now in a better regime, so the next clean probe is a higher peak learning rate. With only 256 steps, this compact CNN may still be under-updating at 1e-3, and AdamW plus warmup makes a moderate increase to 1.5e-3 a plausible way to reduce validation loss further.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous increase in peak learning rate produced a large gain, which suggests the model is still benefiting from more aggressive early optimization under the fixed 256-step budget. The next clean probe is to push the same direction once more, keeping the rest of the now-working optimizer and schedule unchanged.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Learning-rate tuning has delivered most of the gains so far, and the compact model may now be the limiting factor. A single extra conv-pool block is a simple capacity increase that preserves the overall design while giving the network more hierarchical features within the same training recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper three-block layout helped substantially, which suggests the model is still capacity-limited. A modest width increase is the next simple architecture probe: it strengthens all stages without changing the overall training recipe or adding architectural complexity beyond a single scalar.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After the strong gains from increasing feature extractor depth and width, the classifier bottleneck is now unusually small relative to the 1024-dimensional flattened feature map. Widening the hidden layer is a cheap capacity increase that preserves the successful backbone and may convert the richer features into lower validation loss more effectively.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent gains have come from relieving architectural bottlenecks, and the backbone is still fairly small for a 3-block CNN trained with an effective optimizer and schedule. A modest width increase from 16 to 20 is the next simple capacity probe, expanding representational power without changing the overall design or training dynamics.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The strongest improvements have come from relaxing capacity bottlenecks, and after widening the backbone the MLP head is again the narrowest part of the network relative to the 1280-dimensional flattened feature vector. Increasing the hidden layer from 96 to 128 is a cheap follow-up capacity probe that should add little runtime compared with another backbone width jump.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger classifier head regressed, so this candidate restores the better-capacity baseline and uses the step to probe a more aggressive peak learning rate instead. {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(steps_per_epoch // 4, 1), max(total_steps // 20, 1))","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(steps_per_epoch // 2, 1), max(total_steps // 10, 1))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The higher peak learning rate regressed, so this candidate restores the better 2e-3 setting and instead reallocates more of the fixed 256-step budget to full-rate training. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(steps_per_epoch // 2, 1), max(total_steps // 10, 1))","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(steps_per_epoch // 4, 1), max(total_steps // 20, 1))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The shorter warmup regressed, so this candidate restores the better schedule and uses the step to test slightly weaker regularization. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The regularization reduction regressed, so this candidate restores the better optimizer setting and returns to the strongest trend in the run: backbone capacity increases. {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The last width increase regressed, so this candidate restores the stronger width-20 backbone and shifts to a short-horizon optimizer tweak. {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","old":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Recent capacity and optimizer tweaks regressed, so the next probe should preserve the strongest architecture and optimizer settings and only refine budget allocation. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The jump from batch size 64 to 96 produced the largest improvement in the run, which indicates the fixed-step evaluator strongly rewards seeing more examples per update.
